name: Raglan 500
description: A varied Waikato loop with coastal sections, gravel roads, and steady climbing.
text1: This bikepacking route can be done all year through.
Start and finish is in Raglan, home of one of the most world famous left hand surf breaks.
The route goes north and south of Raglan with a decent amount of climbing.
There are not a lot of accommodation and food supply options outside of Raglan, so planning ahead a bit is not a bad idea.
This loop can be done in 2 to 5 days.
text2: You will handle mixed gravel roads, coastal wind, and steady climbing over multiple days. Resupply exists, but the gaps are long enough that some planning is still required.
text3: Start early, carry a weather margin, and keep backup navigation plus an extra food and water buffer. For this 3-day route, we recommend a Gravel Bike to balance comfort and control across mixed terrain. This setup keeps key gear stable and easy to access over long days.
